1200001990.english: Seeing how I can't help but smile whenever I see you, I guess I really like you.
1200001990.korean: 너만 보면 웃음이 나오는 것을 보면 내가 너를 참 좋아하나 봐.

1200001996.english: You want to rent a haka?

1200001998.english: It's too noisy. Let's just standardize the terminology for now.

1200002001.english: I told you she really knows what she's doing.

1200002003.english: Hey! I'm your boss! Why are you ignoring me and following humans instead!
1200002003.korean: 야! 내가 네 대장이거든! 왜 내 말은 무시하고 인간의 말을 따르는 건데!

1200002004.english: Captain. I follow your orders out of loyalty, but you are a human, Commander.
1200002004.korean: 대장. 제가 대장의 말을 따르는 것은 의리 때문이지만 사령관님은 인간님이거든요?

1200002005.english: Did I tell you that I can't refuse if the Commander orders me to defect?
1200002005.korean: 사령관님이 전향하라고 명령하면 저 거부 못한다고 말씀드렸어요?

1200002008.english: So, may I ask to confirm the reason you called us?
1200002008.korean: 그래서 저희를 부르신 이유를 확인차 여쭤봐도 될까요?

1200002012.english: - I explained about the Iron Tower.

1200002013.english: - A structure built by the Iron Bug (Iron Bugs). Its existence has been identified in Europe, and strange phenomena are occurring centered around its location and the Iron Tower.
1200002013.korean: - 철충이 세운 건축물. 그리고 유럽에 그 존재가 파악되었고, 그 위치와 그 철의 탑을 중심으로 이상현상이 발생하고 있다는 것.

1200002014.english: - Also, that they formulated a plan to destroy the Iron Tower for safety.
1200002014.korean: - 안전을 위해서 철의 탑을 파괴하기 위한 작전을 수립했다는 것도.

1200002016.english: I know you're reluctant, but I have no intention of borrowing it for free.
1200002016.korean: 내키지 않는 것은 알지만 공짜로 빌릴 생각은 없어.

1200002017.english: Let's negotiate at a mutually acceptable level.
1200002017.korean: 서로 납득할 수 있는 선에서 협상하자고.

1200002019.english: The operation would be very dangerous, wouldn't it?

1200002020.english: Haha—there's a high risk of the dragon's nest being damaged, right?
1200002020.korean: 하ㅋ- 용의 둥지가 파손될 위험도 크죠?

1200002022.english: My dragon's nest is also an incredibly precious treasure.
1200002022.korean: 내 용의 둥지도 엄청 귀중한 보물이야.

1200002023.english: Where else in the world would you find a treasure like this?
1200002023.korean: 세상에 이런 보물이 또 어디 있겠어?

1200002024.english: It's invisible, undetectable, climbs to high places, moves heavy things well, and... also... uh...
1200002024.korean: 눈에 안 보이지, 감지 안 되지, 높은 곳까지 올라가지, 무거운 것도 잘 옮기지 그리고… 또… 어…

1200002026.english: It is much more precious than things like gold.

1200002027.english: Hey! Don't call it mere gold! It's 24,000 tons!
1200002027.korean: 야! 금 따위라고 하지 마! 2만 4천 톤이라고!

1200002028.english: I'm telling you, the Dragon's Nest is on the verge of breaking down because of that 24,000 tons.
1200002028.korean: 그 2만 4천 톤 때문에 용의 둥지가 고장나기 직전이라고요.

1200002029.english: It exceeds three times the maximum load capacity.

1200002030.english: The Orca technical department inspected it for maintenance this time and said that it wouldn't be surprising if it broke down at any time.
1200002030.korean: 이번에 오르카 기술부 사람들이 정비를 위해 살펴보고는 언제 고장나도 이상할 게 없다고 했다고요.

1200002032.english: Then, let's ask them to repair and reinforce the Dragon's Nest as compensation for this operation.
1200002032.korean: 그, 그러면 이번 작전의 대가로 용의 둥지 수리해주고 강화해달라고 하자.

1200002033.english: For free! Ah, no! Let's ask for gold!
1200002033.korean: 공짜로! 아, 아니! 금 달라고 하자!

1200002036.english: The Dragon's Nest has all sorts of functions, but fundamentally it is an airship, so it is extremely vulnerable to external attacks. There is a possibility that it could be destroyed by even a single direct hit from an anti-aircraft missile.
1200002036.korean: 용의 둥지에 별별 기능이 다 있지만 근본은 비행선이라 외부 공격에 엄청 취약해요. 대공 미사일 하나 제대로 맞아도 파괴될 가능성이 있다고요.

1200002037.english: Don't you remember how we were able to protect that giant airship safely?
1200002037.korean: 우리가 어떻게 저 거대한 비행선을 무사히 지킬 수 있었는지 기억 못 하세요?

1200002038.english: Avoid dangerous areas at all costs, keep cloaking fields and thermal and radio stealth devices turned on at all times, stay at high altitudes normally, and if you need to descend, make sure it is safe before coming down.
1200002038.korean: 위험한 곳은 무조건 피하고, 은폐장과 열, 전파 스텔스 장치는 항상 켜놓고, 평소에는 고고도에서 머무르고, 내려올 일이 있으면 무조건 안전 확인하고 내려오고.

1200002039.english: We went through all sorts of hardships to protect this, and you want us to send it onto the battlefield?
1200002039.korean: 온갖 고생 다 하면서 지켰는데 이걸 전장에 들이밀자고요?

1200002040.english: If it breaks, just ask them to make a new one!

1200002041.english: You're saying we should recreate the pinnacle of lost technology, made by a mad engineer on the Black River who received unlimited funding and technology just before humanity was wiped out?
1200002041.korean: 인류가 멸망하기 직전에 블랙리버에서 무제한의 예산과 기술을 지원받은 미친 공학자가 만든 로스트 테크놀로지의 결정체를 다시 만들자고요?

1200002042.english: Do you think it would be easy?

1200002043.english: - Sterling did not object directly, but indirectly expressed strong resistance.
1200002043.korean: - 스털링은 직접적인 반대는 안 했지만 간접적으로 심한 거부감을 드러냈다.

1200002044.english: Can you make this new in Orca?
1200002044.korean: 오르카에서 이걸 새로 만들 수 있어?

1200002045.english: I won't lie. It's impossible. We have the technical team, so even if we manage to restore it somehow, we lack the budget and manpower.
1200002045.korean: 거짓말은 안 할 게. 무리야. 기술은 우리 기술진들이 있으니 어떻게든 복원해도 예산과 인력이 부족해.

1200002046.english: Because there are many places where the budget is needed and manpower is required.
1200002046.korean: 예산 쓸 곳, 인력이 필요한 곳이 많으니까.

1200002049.english: I guess I'll have to get a lot of gold for that?

1200002052.english: - Sterling cursed and left the room.

1200002053.english: - I tried to follow but stopped.

1200002054.english: - It is a matter between Fafnir and her subordinate, and if I, a human, get involved, it will become more complicated.
1200002054.korean: - 파프니르와 그녀의 부하 사이의 일이고, 인간인 내가 끼어들면 더 복잡해지기 때문이다.

1200002055.english: So, how much do you want?

1200002061.english: Then let's unify it as the Dragon's Nest instead of Haka.
1200002061.korean: 그러면 하카가 아니라 용의 둥지로 통일하자.

1200002062.english: I'd like to borrow the dragon's nest.

1200002063.english: It's needed for this military operation.

1200002064.english: Do you know the Iron Tower?

1200002065.english: The dragon's nest is essential for that operation.

1200002068.english: I won't lie. There's a high risk of it breaking.
1200002068.korean: 거짓말은 안 할게. 파손될 위험이 커.

1200002070.english: If you sigh, happiness will run away.

1200002071.english: It's the opposite... I'm sighing because I'm unhappy right now.

1200002072.english: You sigh a lot too, don't you?
1200002072.korean: 너도 평소 한숨 내쉴 때가 많잖아.

1200002075.english: What's weighing so heavily on your mind?

1200002077.english: No. I guess I just have to accept it now.

1200002081.english: At least say, "We couldn't advance because there was no road." I won't let you say that.
1200002081.korean: 적어도 ‘길이 없어서 진격을 못했습니다.’ 라는 말은 못하게 해줄게.

1200002082.english: Steel Line is not a unit that stops advancing just because there's no path...
1200002082.korean: 스틸라인이 길이 없다고 진격을 멈추는 부대는 아니지만...

1200002083.english: Hearing that makes me feel reassured.

1200002084.english: And I will protect the engineers until they reach the Iron Tower.
1200002084.korean: 그리고 철의 탑에 도달할 때까지 공병들을 지켜주겠다.

1200002085.english: Because if the Air Force can't destroy the target, they'll have to bring down the Iron Tower from the ground.
1200002085.korean: 공군이 목표를 파괴하지 못하면 지상에서 철의 탑을 무너트려야 하니까.

1200002086.english: I brought a whole bunch of Azaz's special bag bombs, but...
1200002086.korean: 아자즈 특제 가방 폭탄을 잔뜩 가져오기는 했는데…

1200002087.english: I wonder if something like this would really bring me down...

1200002089.english: Not that you added an explosive function to another object?
1200002089.korean: 다른 물건에 폭발 기능을 넣은 게 아니라?

1200002091.english: Why? Why are you distancing yourself? Anxiously?

1200002092.english: Because I saw the rocket-engine-powered toddler ride-on toy Azaz made blow up an entire factory!
1200002092.korean: 아자즈가 만든 로켓엔진 달린 유아용 탈거리가 공장 하나를 통째로 날려버리는 것을 봤으니까!

1200002093.english: And my room has been broken into several times too!
1200002093.korean: 그리고 내 방도 여러 번 터졌으니까!

1200002094.english: So, that Azaz made a bomb for the explosion?
1200002094.korean: 그런 아자즈가 폭발을 위한 폭탄을 만들었다고?

1200002098.english: It makes me happy to see you so joyful.

1200002099.english: Do I look like I'm happy to you!?
1200002099.korean: 너희 눈에는 내가 기뻐하는 것처럼 보여!?

1200002101.english: We just wanted Draculina to smile...
1200002101.korean: 저희는 그냥 드라큐리나가 웃기를 바랐을 뿐인데…

1200002102.english: If you want to make me laugh, think properly!
1200002102.korean: 나 웃게 만들고 싶으면 제대로 고민하라고!

1200002103.english: Oh, would you like me to remodel your room again?

1200002104.english: No! Ugh! And if you remodel your room again without my permission, you're dead!
1200002104.korean: 아니라고! 우씨! 또 내 허락 없이 방 개조하면 죽을 줄 알아!

1200002105.english: If you die, I'll bury you with me.

1200002107.english: Um... it's okay if it's already been modified, right?

1200002108.english: Already? You modified it again without me knowing!?
1200002108.korean: 이미? 나 모르는 사이에 또 개조했어!?

1200002111.english: I really put my heart and soul into making this bomb.
1200002111.korean: 이 폭탄 정말 심혈을 기울여서 만들었어요.

1200002112.english: With the help of Mr. T-40 Hyena from the Horde and Mr. C-11 Scarabya, I was able to maximize its power.
1200002112.korean: 호드의 하이에나 씨랑 스카라비아 씨의 도움을 받아서 위력을 최대한으로 끌어올렸죠.

1200002113.english: Mr. T-40 Hyena was having a hard time because he was in the brig, but thanks to being temporarily released for this operation, we were able to finish it on time.
1200002113.korean: 하이에나 씨가 영창에 있어서 힘들었지만 이번 작전을 위해 임시 방면된 덕분에 제시간 내에 완성 시킬 수 있었어요.

1200002114.english: And if you light it like this...

1200002116.english: I was so surprised I thought I was going to die...

1200002117.english: When I die, please bury me in the place where my master will be buried.
1200002117.korean: 제가 죽으면 주인님이 묻히실 자리에 미리 묻어주세요.

1200002119.english: Huh? I want to show you how beautiful the colors of the flames are.

1200002120.english: Look. Isn't it a beautiful rainbow color? Even when it explodes, it will explode beautifully.
1200002120.korean: 보세요. 예쁜 무지개 색이죠? 폭발할 때도 예쁘게 폭발할 거예요.

1200002121.english: Are you crazy? Lighting a bomb in our own base!
1200002121.korean: 미쳤어? 아군 진지에서 폭탄에 불을 붙이다니!

1200002122.english: Don't worry. If you don't detonate it with this special detonator, it'll just catch fire like this.
1200002122.korean: 걱정 마세요. 이 특수 뇌관으로 터트리지 않으면 이렇게 불만 붙고 마니까요.

1200002123.english: Isn't a bomb only a bomb if it explodes when you want it to?
1200002123.korean: 폭탄은 원할 때 터져야 폭탄 아닌가요?

1200002125.english: Include me in the list of people who want an explosion. Huh? Include me.
1200002125.korean: 폭발 원하는 사람에 나 좀 포함시켜라. 응? 나 포함시키라고.

1200002126.english: Does Draculina want to blow something up too? Send a bunch to my room later.
1200002126.korean: 드라큐리나도 뭔가 폭파시키고 싶으신가요? 나중에 방에 잔뜩 보내-

1200002127.english: Don't burst it unless I want you to!

1200002128.english: I'm so frustrated I could die!

1200002129.english: Grumpy! Don't react to the word "burst"! Gloomy! Don't react to the word "death"!
1200002129.korean: 괴팍이! 터진다는 말에 반응하지마! 음침이! 죽는다는 말에 반응하지마!

1200002130.english: As expected, Draculina. You know us well.

1200002131.english: That means you're that close, right?

1200002132.english: But what about the eccentric? That's the first time I've ever heard that title. That's interesting.
1200002132.korean: 그런데 괴팍이? 태어나서 처음으로 들어보는 호칭이네요. 흥미로워요.

1200002133.english: Haha, it feels like we've become closer now that we've given each other nicknames and call each other by them.
1200002133.korean: 후후, 별명을 붙여주고 불러주니 더 친해진 기분이네요.

1200002134.english: If it's Draculina's country, I don't mind being buried in the next tomb, even if it's not the same one.
1200002134.korean: 드라큐리나라면 같은 관은 아니지만 옆 무덤에 묻혀도 좋아요.

1200002135.english: Oh, of course, you can bury Azaz on the other side.
1200002135.korean: 아, 물론 그 반대쪽에는 아자즈가 묻혀도 좋아요.

1200002136.english: I want to be buried next to Draculina.
1200002136.korean: 저는 드라큐리나 옆에 묻히고 싶은 걸요.

1200002137.english: That sounds good too. Just like now.

1200002138.english: So, even if I die, I can't escape these guys...

1200002139.english: I'm glad you were so moved.
1200002139.korean: 그렇게 감동하신 것을 보니 저도 기뻐요.

1200002146.english: It feels weird that you answered first...
1200002146.korean: 네가 먼저 대답하니까 뭔가 분위기가 이상하잖아…

1200002147.english: What kind of picnic is this...?

1200002148.english: Uroborus! How many snacks are allowed?

1200002150.english: Are you making fun of me right now?

1200002151.english: Heh heh, it's inevitable to get tense during a battle, but isn't it better to be relaxed before the fight?
1200002151.korean: 후후, 전투 중에 긴장하는 건 어쩔 수 없어도, 전투 전에는 편한 마음으로 있는 게 좋지 않나?

1200002152.english: EB-48G Hraesvelgr, please give us a briefing.

1200002154.english: The objective of this operation is the destruction of the Iron Tower.
1200002154.korean: 이번 작전의 목표는 철의 탑의 파괴입니다.

1200002155.english: The destruction of the Iron Tower will involve three main methods.
1200002155.korean: 철의 탑 파괴는 크게 세 가지 방법이 동원될 것입니다.
